dublinCore.dc:title: 119 SRES 773 IS: Recognizing Father’s Day and the role a father plays in the lives of children and adults.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mrs. Moody submitted the following resolution; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: Recognizing Father’s Day and the role a father plays in the lives of children and adults.

whereas.text: Whereas Father’s Day is celebrated on the third Sunday of June and will be celebrated in the United States on Sunday, June 21, 2026;
Whereas fathers play an important role in the lives of their children by improving their—
Whereas children with present or engaged fathers are—

paragraph.text: less likely to repeat a grade;
more likely to enter college or find stable employment after high school;
less likely to go to jail;
less likely to experience a teen birth; and
less likely to experience mental health disorders;

whereas.text: Whereas children without present or engaged fathers—

paragraph.text: represent the vast majority of runaway children;
represent the vast majority of juveniles in State-operated institutions;
are more likely to have children outside of marriage or partnership;
are more likely to be involved in gangs; and
are much more likely to live in poverty; and

whereas.text: Whereas the genuine love of a father is irreplaceable, and the positive outcomes generated by the love and support of a father prove both the irreplaceable role a father fills and the responsibility fathers have in caring for their children and families: Now, therefore, be it

paragraph.text: acknowledges the importance of efforts by fathers to engage and to provide continuous physical and emotional support to children and families;
acknowledges that sustained engagement by a father throughout the life of a child is a privileged responsibility that fulfills the father as much as the child; and
wishes all fathers and father figures in the United States a happy Father’s Day.
